finalAssistantMessages.text: The UI now passes all 10 browser scenarios and the production build under Node 18. Both default screenshots are collapsed, fit the viewport, have no horizontal overflow or broken images, and the expanded controls still produce the same API payloads. I’m using the GitHub publishing workflow now to commit this focused `geesome-ui` slice and open the PR.

One last coverage gap remained: the direct blocker-to-repair path existed but wasn’t exercised because the fixture always reported verified ownership. I’m making the fixture reflect the submitted proof token so the test can show an unverified reason, open the ownership group through the repair action, then retry successfully.

Opened ready-to-review PR: [geesome-ui#29](https://github.com/galtproject/geesome-ui/pull/29) It simplifies social migration using collapsed advanced sections, keeps primary actions visible, and provides a direct ownership-repair path. All 10 e2e screenshot tests and the production build pass under Node 18. PR is currently mergeable. Commit: `6df8604` ::git-create-branch{cwd="/Users/microwavedev/workspace/microwave-hub/geesome-ui" branch="codex/activitypub-simplified-migration-ui"} ::git-stage{cwd="/Users/microwavedev/workspace/microwave-hub/geesome-ui"} ::git-commit{cwd="/Users/microwavedev/workspace/microwave-hub/geesome-ui"} ::git-push{cwd="/Users/microwavedev/workspace/microwave-hub/gees
